title: Sentinel Shield recovery inspection report
description: Machine-readable output of `scripts/recover-operation.sh --target <dir> --inspect --format json`. A READ-ONLY snapshot of the interrupted-operation state a consuming project is in, computed with the SAME library validators the real recovery path uses (so the report can never disagree with what recovery would do). Exit code accompanies it: 0 when the journal is consistent, 4 when it is tampered/partial. Contains NO secrets and NO repo-local absolute paths beyond the caller-supplied target.

schema.description: Fixed discriminator for this report shape.

target.description: Canonical consuming-project directory that was inspected.

state.description: The operation-lock's recorded state-machine position, or null when no lock is present.

lock_owner.description: Ownership of the current lock, or null when no (schema-valid) lock is present.

pid.description: Owning PID recorded in the lock (null when absent).

token.description: PID-independent ownership token (null for a legacy lock without one).

hostname.description: Host that acquired the lock (null for a legacy lock without one).

journal_valid.description: True when the append-only transaction journal chain verifies strictly (no non-JSON/partial line, no missing/ill-typed field, no unsafe path, intact seq+prev+hash). Absent journal counts as valid.

safe_to_resume.description: True when `--resume-rollback` is expected to exit 0: either the lock is already 'completed' (clearing only), or a rollback is safe AND the journal is intact enough to resume (a torn trailing line is tolerated).

safe_to_rollback.description: True when an interrupted operation can be rolled back safely: schema-valid lock, target match, contained+present snapshot dir, and a present touched manifest. False for a completed lock (nothing to roll back) or any missing precondition.

required_manual_actions.description: Human-actionable blockers that prevent a fully-automatic safe recovery. Empty when recovery can proceed unattended.
